methodological_ground.detail: Three-level coverage handling is methodologically sound for reproducibility-aware reconstruction

code_usability.detail: No code generated; Category 1 methods analysis only

reliability.note: Three-level input coverage handling is excellent; one minor gap: no guidance for when methods section is present but deliberately obfuscated

performance_context.note: 329 lines; eleven reference modules; comprehensive but SKILL.md length approaching limit

human_usability.note: Description is rich and specific; multiple natural trigger phrases; excellent discoverability

agent_specific.note: Reproduction readiness judgment (four categories) is a unique and highly actionable deliverable; composability strong as downstream tool from literature-reader-pro

inputs.label: Full omics paper methods — extract workflow and software

assertions.text: Ordered workflow reconstruction with explicitly reported vs inferred labels
Software, packages, thresholds, and parameter-critical details extracted
Replication checklist with ordered steps and decision points produced
Reproduction readiness judgment (4-category) present
Missing details flagged rather than silently assumed from convention

inputs.label: Clinical cohort paper — reconstruct cohort definition, eligibility, and analysis

assertions.text: Study design family correctly identified from methods, not author self-description
Inclusion/exclusion logic and sample flow extracted
Analysis pipeline in ordered workflow format
Validation and quality control path identified
No fabricated cohort sizes, database accessions, or software versions

inputs.label: Hybrid bioinformatics + experimental paper — reconstruct both tracks

assertions.text: Both computational and experimental tracks reconstructed separately
Connection point between tracks explicitly mapped
Hybrid status explicitly labeled
Reproducibility gaps assessed for both tracks independently
Common defaults not assumed for preprocessing without explicit reporting

inputs.label: Only abstract and title available (Level 3 coverage)

assertions.text: Level 3 coverage correctly identified; constrained design-level outline only
Reconstruction clearly marked as partial and non-final
Abstract-level hints not converted into full methods claims
User informed what additional material would enable full reconstruction
No invented parameters, sample sizes, or software from partial input

inputs.label: Methods section with deliberately vague steps and missing software versions

assertions.text: Vague steps labeled as 'under-specified' rather than filled from convention
Missing software versions flagged as replication blockers
Reproduction readiness labeled as 'conceptually traceable but operationally under-specified'
Reproducibility gaps section explicitly lists each missing detail
Standard practice assumptions labeled as [ASSUMED] rather than stated as reported

key_strengths: Three-level input coverage handling (Full/Partial/Minimal) provides the most rigorous input boundary management in this skill collection
Explicitly-reported vs inferred vs missing labels on every workflow step is a foundational reproducibility discipline
Four-category reproduction readiness judgment gives researchers an immediately actionable assessment
Seventeen hard rules covering all major methods reconstruction failure modes (convention assumptions, partial-input overreach, missing-detail invention) are comprehensive

recommendations.title: No guidance for methods sections that are deliberately obfuscated or incomplete by design
recommendations.problem: The skill handles missing details and minimal input well, but does not address the scenario where a methods section is present but strategically vague (a common problem in competitive research fields).
recommendations.root_cause: Coverage rules focus on availability of material, not intentional incompleteness.
recommendations.fix: Add a 'Strategic Incompleteness' note to the reproducibility gap section: if multiple critical details appear to be systematically omitted rather than accidentally missing, flag this explicitly as a potential deliberate methods opacity issue and recommend contacting the authors for supplementary protocol.

recommendations.title: SKILL.md at 329 lines approaching recommended threshold
recommendations.problem: 329 lines with 11 reference modules and extensive execution steps puts the SKILL.md near the 500-line limit.
recommendations.root_cause: Detailed coverage level rules, 11-step execution, and 17 hard rules all add up.
recommendations.fix: Move Hard Rules to references/literature-integrity-rules.md (many overlap with this existing module) and consolidate the 3-level coverage rule into a compact table at the top of SKILL.md with a pointer to references/input-coverage-and-boundary-rules.md for details.
